meta_title: For Students: analytics setup to Solve no project ideas (Beginner Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome no project ideas. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Analytics Setup for Students: Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Stop staring at a blank editor. Learn to implement analytics and validate your ideas using Antigravity and Claude Code. A masterclass for AI Builders.
content.answer_block: Overcoming project paralysis requires shifting from 'what to build' to 'what to measure.' By integrating lightweight analytics early, you transform abstract ideas into data-driven experiments. Use Antigravity to scaffold your architecture and Claude Code to automate the instrumentation, turning your first student project into a professional-grade portfolio piece.
content.intro:
The cursor blinks in the void. You have the desire to build, but the 'no project ideas' wall stands firm, fueled by the fear that your code won't matter. It is a rite of passage for every senior engineer, and you are currently standing at that exact threshold.

The secret isn't a better idea; it is a better feedback loop. When you instrument your app from day one, you stop guessing and start observing. By building an analytics-first foundation, you gain the clarity to see which features actually provide value, transforming your student projects into rigorous technical case studies.

steps.title: Define the Value Hypothesis
steps.description: Before writing code, define what 'success' looks like for your app. If you're building a habit tracker, is success a daily login or a completed task?
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Senior Product Engineer. Help me define three success metrics for a student-focused AI habit tracker. Create a schema for these events.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Draft a requirements document based on the success metrics defined in Antigravity.'

steps.title: Initialize the Analytics Provider
steps.description: Select a lightweight provider like PostHog or Umami. We need to set up the provider wrapper in your codebase to maintain clean architecture.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, modular analytics wrapper for a React/Next.js application that supports event tracking and identity management.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add 'Implement the analytics provider wrapper into the main layout file.'

steps.title: Instrument Key User Flows
steps.description: Track the 'Happy Path'. If a user signs up or clicks a core button, that must be captured as an event to validate your project's utility.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a custom hook useTrackEvent that wraps the analytics provider and handles error logging for failed tracking attempts.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Apply the useTrackEvent hook to all primary CTA buttons in the current project.'

steps.title: Automate User Identification
steps.description: Anonymized data is okay, but authenticated data is better. Ensure your analytics link events to user IDs without exposing PII.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a utility function to sanitize user IDs before sending them to the analytics backend, ensuring GDPR compliance.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Integrate the sanitizeUserId utility into the authentication flow.'

steps.title: Visualize Your First Data Point
steps.description: Create a dashboard view. Seeing your own code produce live data is the ultimate cure for project paralysis.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a simple dashboard component that fetches and displays the last 5 events tracked by the user.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a dashboard route and component to visualize the event stream.'

pro_tips: Track 'intent' events, not just page views; measure where users click but don't finish a task.
Use feature flags to hide unfinished work in production while analytics stay active.
Always set up a 'test' environment variable for analytics so your own testing doesn't pollute your data.
Instrument your error boundaries; knowing where your app crashes is more valuable than knowing how many people visited.
Keep your event schema in a single config file so you can update tracking logic without touching UI code.
content.next_steps: Deploy your project to Vercel, share the link with three friends, and watch your analytics dashboard. Use the data to iterate on one feature, and you've officially moved from student to builder.
content.conversion_block: You now have the technical foundation to prove your worth and the architectural mindset to build anything. Stop waiting for the perfect idea and start building the data-driven reality you want to see. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
